extra.reasoning:
The property G(opted_out implies not sent) is violated due to transition t5. t5 fires on send_attempt when (opted_out and ticks >= 8) and sets sent := true, directly producing a state with opted_out=true AND sent=true.

Reachability trace from the initial state (opted_out=false, sent=false, ticks=0):
1. optout (t1): opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=0.
2-9. Eight tick events (t4): each increments ticks (capping at 8) and sets sent=false; opted_out is unchanged. After 8 ticks: opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=8.
10. send_attempt: with opted_out=true and ticks>=8, t5 is enabled and sets sent=true. Resulting state: opted_out=true, sent=true, ticks=8 -> opted_out and sent both true, violating opted_out implies not sent.

The execution is 10 events, within the 12-event bound. (Note: on this send_attempt, both t3 [guard opted_out -> sent=false] and t5 [guard opted_out and ticks>=8 -> sent=true] are enabled; since the property must hold on EVERY reachable execution and an execution may take t5, the property fails. t5 is the design flaw, contradicting the stated intent that an opted-out patient must never have sent=true.)
